back_translation: And David again gathered all the chosen of Israel, thirty thousand.

And David arose and went, he and all the people who were with him, from Baale of Judah, to bring up from there the ark of God, which is called by the Name — the name of the LORD of hosts who is enthroned of the cherubim upon it.
And they set the ark of God on a new cart, and brought it up from the house of Abinadab which was on the hill; and Uzzah and Ahio, sons of Abinadab, were driving the new cart.
And they brought it up from the house of Abinadab which was on the hill, with the ark of God; and Ahio walked before the ark.
And David and all the house of Israel were celebrating before the LORD with all kinds of wood of cypress, and with lyres and with harps and with timbrels and with rattles and with cymbals.
And they came to the threshing floor of Nacon, and Uzzah reached out to the ark of God and took hold of it, because the oxen had stumbled.
And the anger of the LORD burned against Uzzah, and God struck him there for the irreverence, and he died there beside the ark of God.
And David became angry over the LORD's bursting out against Uzzah, and he called that place Perez-uzzah, to this day.
And David feared the LORD that day, and said, 'How shall the ark of the LORD come to me?'
And David was not willing to bring the ark of the LORD aside to himself to the City of David, and David turned it aside to the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.
And the ark of the LORD stayed in the house of Obed-edom the Gittite three months, and the LORD blessed Obed-edom and all his house.

And it was told to King David, saying, 'The LORD has blessed the house of Obed-edom and all that belongs to him, because of the ark of God.' And David went and brought up the ark of God from the house of Obed-edom into the City of David with rejoicing.
And it happened, when those bearing the ark of the LORD had advanced six paces, that he sacrificed an ox and a fattened calf.
And David was dancing with all might before the LORD; and David was girded with a linen ephod.
And David and all the house of Israel were bringing up the ark of the LORD with shouting and with the sound of the ram's horn.

And it happened, as the ark of the LORD came into the City of David, that Michal daughter of Saul looked down through the window, and saw King David leaping and dancing before the LORD; and she despised him in her heart.
And they brought in the ark of the LORD and set it in its place in the midst of the tent that David had pitched for it; and David offered burnt offerings before the LORD and peace offerings.
And David finished offering the burnt offering and the peace offerings, and he blessed the people in the name of the LORD of hosts.
And he distributed to all the people, to all the multitude of Israel, both man and woman, to each one a loaf of bread, and a date cake, and a raisin cake; and all the people went, each to his house.

And David returned to bless his house. And Michal daughter of Saul went out to meet David, and said, 'How honorable today the king of Israel has been — who exposed himself today in the eyes of the maidservants of his servants, as one of the empty ones would shamelessly expose himself!'
And David said to Michal, 'Before the LORD, who chose me from your father and from all his house, to appoint me ruler over the people of the LORD, over Israel — I will dance before the LORD.
And I will make myself even more contemptible than this, and I will be low in my own eyes; but with the maidservants of whom you have spoken — with them I will be honored.'
And Michal daughter of Saul had no child to the day of her death.
